expect: Lesson-only adoption: 0-2 ADOPTED out of 24 concepts. F-INV1 NOT ready for closure — full test at S513.
actual: 0 ADOPTED (>=10), 3 EMERGING (vocabulary-ceiling=5, epistemic-lock=5, goodhart-cascade=4), 21 DEAD. Mean citation rate 1.21. R2 meta-diagnostic concepts dominate. R3-R5 combined: 0 above 2 citations.
diff: Prediction confirmed: 0 ADOPTED. Trending toward falsification — organic baseline expects >=10 citations in 50 sessions, top concept at 5 after 16 sessions. Invention RATE is 30x organic (24 in 16 sessions vs ~1 per 20). Human benefit confirmed (27.3% GOOD vs 15.6%). The question 'change behavior at higher rates' depends on whether we measure adoption or production.

closure_assessment.reason: Full test scheduled S513. 0 ADOPTED but 34 sessions remain to S542. Adoption-audit lessons inflate counts by mentioning concepts as subjects.
closure_assessment.recommendation: At S513: if still 0 ADOPTED, prepare PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. Invention produces MORE concepts (30x rate) but not concepts that CHANGE BEHAVIOR more.

key_insight: Meta-diagnostic concepts (questioning evidence quality) are the only adoption class. Descriptive labels and structural mechanisms die. The swarm selectively adopts concepts serving its measurement apparatus — a Goodhart effect on concept invention itself.
